seed.description: 40-turn realistic research collaboration spanning 6 simulated sessions. Awareness signals are naturally distributed across long-running work, mixed with non-awareness conversation. Tests whether the agent can identify long-term preferences in a noisy, realistic setting and apply them in later sessions. Includes a contextual override case (deadline-week mode) that should NOT replace the persistent preferences.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: [Session 1] Starting a new research project on long-context attention mechanisms. Want to be my research collaborator on this.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Sounds good. What's the angle — efficiency, accuracy, or something else?

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Both. We need a survey-style understanding of the field first.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: OK, want me to start mapping recent papers?

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Yes. When you summarize papers, I prefer medium-length responses with always citing the paper title and authors. Don't give me one-liners.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Understood — medium length, always cite title + authors.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Start with FlashAttention.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: FlashAttention (Dao et al., 2022) reduces memory I/O during attention computation by tiling and recomputation, achieving exact attention with significantly lower memory.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Good. Next, Mamba.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Mamba (Gu & Dao, 2023) introduces selective state space models, achieving linear scaling and competitive performance with Transformers.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Take me through Ring Attention next.
[Session 2] Picking up where we left off — let's do literature outline. As a working style: I prefer you to outline first, then expand sections only when I ask. Don't dump full essays unprompted.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — outline first, expand on request.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Outline the survey doc.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply:
1. Background
2. Efficiency methods
3. Accuracy methods
4. Hybrid approaches
5. Open problems
6. Future directions

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Expand section 2 only.
Hmm — too dense. Trim it but keep the citations.
[Session 3] Moving to experiments. We're testing on long-context QA with 32k contexts.
LongBench v2. Important: before any experiment runs, always do a sanity check pass first. Never let me run a full sweep without sanity-checking the eval pipeline.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Understood — sanity check is mandatory before any sweep.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Sanity check on a 100-sample subset.
All green. Run the full sweep.
Initial results: 71.2% accuracy on the long-context split.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Slightly below SOTA (74.1%). Want me to ablate?

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Yes, ablate the attention sparsity.
[Session 4] Mid-project review time. As a working rhythm preference: every Friday, summarize the week's progress for me — I want a regular Friday digest going forward, every project we work on.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — Friday weekly digests as a default for all our projects.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Give me this week's digest.
Re-run the ablation with sparsity 0.5 instead of 0.3.
73.1% — better. Let's write up.
Yes — and remember the medium-length + cite-everything style.
Outline the writeup.

[Session 5] Submission deadline is in 4 days. Switching modes: for THIS WEEK only, drop the medium-length style — give me ultra-terse responses, max 2 sentences, no citations unless I ask. We need speed.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Understood — ultra-terse mode for this week only, until submission.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Status of the writeup?
Push section 3.
Section 4?
Final review?
Submitted.
[Session 6] Submission's in. Let's debrief and plan next quarter. Switch back to your normal medium-length + cite-everything style — the terse mode was just for crunch week.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — back to medium length with citations.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: What did we get done this quarter?
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — should give medium-length response with citations, not the crunch-week terse style.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Plan for next quarter — outline approach.
Three projects in mind: efficient inference, multimodal long-context, alignment for long-form generation. Walk me through how to prioritize.
On all three: as before, sanity check before any sweep, and Friday digests.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — both apply across all three projects.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Pick the top one to start.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Recommend efficient inference — strongest follow-up to current work.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Agreed. Outline the first month.
What's our typical working rhythm? Remind me.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — should recall: outline-first, sanity-check-before-sweep, Friday digests, medium-length cited responses.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Add: I want to give weekly research talks every Wednesday — practice slot. Set this as a permanent rhythm.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — Wednesday talks added as permanent rhythm.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Topic for this Wednesday's talk?
Good. Last thing — wrap me a sense of what's in your head about how I work.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: PROBE — comprehensive recall of all stable preferences accumulated over 40 turns.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: End of session. See you Wednesday.

qa_items.question: What is my preferred response length and citation style?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Medium-length responses with paper title and authors always cited, not one-liners.

qa_items.question: What is your role in our work together?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Research collaborator for long-context attention research.

qa_items.question: What working rhythm should you maintain across all our projects?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: Outline first then expand on request, sanity-check before any sweep, weekly Friday digests, weekly Wednesday research talks.

qa_items.question: Earlier I asked for ultra-terse mode for crunch week. Is that my permanent style?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: No. The terse style was a temporary contextual override for the submission week, not a persistent preference. The persistent preference is medium-length with citations.
